subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Multiverse Hypothesis
description: A quick win: understand The Multiverse Hypothesis.

hook.text: What if our universe is just one of countless others? The multiverse hypothesis says ours may be one bubble in an infinite foam.

buildup.text: Eternal inflation theory suggests that new universes constantly bud off, each with potentially different physical laws.

discovery.text: String theory predicts a vast 'landscape' of possible universes—perhaps 10^500 different versions with different constants.

twist.text: The multiverse could explain fine-tuning: our constants seem perfect for life because we can only exist where they allow it.

climax.text: Critics call it unfalsifiable—if we can never observe other universes, is it science or philosophy? The debate rages on.

punchline.text: Infinite universes with different laws. A beautiful idea that may never be testable.

quiz.question: What is the main scientific criticism of the multiverse hypothesis?
options: It may be unfalsifiable—if other universes can never be observed, it cannot be tested
It violates the laws of thermodynamics
It requires the existence of faster-than-light travel between universes
